name: Italy
summary: Italy ranges from cool northern regions to warm Mediterranean south, with major regional diversity and many important indigenous grapes.

trentino-alto-adige.name: Trentino-Alto Adige
trentino-alto-adige.summary: Cool Alpine north-eastern Italian region producing fresh aromatic whites and some elegant reds, with Alto Adige and Trentino as key study names.
trentino-alto-adige.styleSummary: Fresh aromatic whites, good acidity and some refined sparkling or lighter red styles.
examTips: Think Alpine freshness, altitude and strong diurnal range.
Alto Adige and Trentino are the key names to place here.
Pinot Grigio, Chardonnay and aromatic whites are central study themes.

Pinot Grigio.summary: Fresh light to medium-bodied white grape producing pear and citrus styles in cooler sites.
Pinot Grigio.style: Light to medium-bodied white with pear, citrus and subtle stone fruit notes.
examTips: Usually fresher and more precise here than in hotter regions.

Sauvignon Blanc.summary: Aromatic white grape producing citrus, herb and green fruit notes with good freshness.
Sauvignon Blanc.style: Fresh aromatic whites with citrus, herb and green fruit notes.
winemaking: Usually stainless steel to preserve aromatics
examTips: Think freshness and aromatic purity.

Chardonnay.summary: Versatile white grape showing fresh citrus and stone fruit, sometimes with subtle oak.
Chardonnay.style: Fresh citrus to stone-fruit styles, sometimes with subtle oak.
examTips: Usually fresher and lighter than richer warm-climate Chardonnay.

veneto.name: Veneto

Corvina.summary: Key red grape of Veneto, giving red cherry fruit, freshness and medium body, especially in Valpolicella styles.

examTips: Think red cherry and freshness in basic styles.

Pinot Grigio.summary: Widely planted white grape usually made in a light fresh pear-and-citrus style.

winemaking: Usually made in youthful fruit-driven styles

prosecco-doc.name: Prosecco DOC

conegliano-valdobbiadene-prosecco-superi.name: Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG
